remember: Explain the basic elements of a report, including introduction, body, and conclusion.
Identify the key features of a good report, such as clarity, concision, and objectivity.
List the different types of reports, including formal and informal reports.
understand: Describe the purpose of a report and its importance in communication.
Explain the role of the reader in a report and how to tailor the content accordingly.
Understand the concept of audience analysis and its application in report writing.
apply: Write a simple report using the basic elements of a report.
Use clear and concise language to communicate findings and recommendations in a report.
Organize a report in a logical and coherent manner, using headings, subheadings, and bullet points.
analyze: Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of a given report and suggest improvements.
Evaluate the effectiveness of a report in achieving its purpose, considering factors such as clarity, concision, and objectivity.
Compare and contrast different types of reports, identifying their unique features and applications.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, by the end of this chapter, students will be able to write a report on a given topic, using the basic elements of a report.
Students will understand the importance of a good report in communication and be able to identify its key features.
Students will be able to analyze a report and suggest improvements, considering factors such as clarity, concision, and objectivity.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE will test students' ability to write a clear and concise report, using proper formatting and organization.
Students will be tested on their understanding of the purpose of a report and its importance in communication.
CBSE will assess students' ability to analyze a report and evaluate its effectiveness in achieving its purpose.
